events.title: Exclusive showing:  'The Day Iceland Stood Still'
events.description:
Event date: May 21, 2026 
Event Time: 05:30 PM - 08:00 PM
Location: 
300 Hillside Avenue
Needham, MA 02494
Description:
Progressive Needham and the Center at the Heights are pleased to present an exclusive screening of the award-winning documentary, The Day Iceland Stood Still.  The film is an iconic depiction of a successful social strike.  The day in 1975 that 90% of women in Iceland ‘took the day off’ rocked the nation and began a movement that has propelled the country from the bottom to the top of world gender equality indexes.  The documentary uses personal stories and animation to show how this massive, humorous, and urgent act of solidarity launched Iceland into a global leader of gender equality.  Stay for a conversation with Fredie Kay from the Women’s History Center and Deb Paul from Neighborhood United Indivisible following the film.

events.title: 2026 Needham High School Civics Showcase
events.description:
Event date: May 30, 2026 
Event Time: 02:00 PM - 03:30 PM
Location: 
1471 Highland Avenue
Needham, MA 02492
Description:
The League of Women Voters of Needham and the Needham High School Social Studies Department are co-sponsoring Students Advocate for Change, the Needham High School Civics Showcase, on Saturday, May 30 at 2-3:30 PM at Powers Hall, Needham Town Hall.All high school students in MA are required to select a state or local issue and prepare an advocacy plan. At the Showcase, more than 100 eleventh grade students in 50 groups will share their research, materials, and positions in a science fair type setting.The public is encouraged to come and talk with the next generation of policy advocates about the issues important to them. Visit www.lwv-needham.org for details.

events.title: How To Build An Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) in Needham
events.description: How to Build an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) in Needham Join us to get your questions answered! This event—co-sponsored by the Needham Housing Coalition, Charles River Regional Chamber, Progressive Needham, League of Women Voters, and Green Needham—will explore why and how to add an ADU to your property. Date & Time: Wednesday, May 20th, 7 p.m. Location: Needham Public Library   How can you benefit from an ADU? How do you build and finance an ADU? What are the new ADU bylaw requirements?   ADUs are a valuable small housing opportunity for young people, the local workforce, seniors who are downsizing, and property owners seeking supplemental income. Recent state initiatives increase the opportunity for single family homeowners to build ADUs.   Learn from our panel of experts: Jim Sullivan, Needham Building Department Amy Wortzman, Eastern Bank Rick Leland, Remodeling remedies, LLC Oscar Mertz, Needham Planning Board Needham resident who has built an ADU Admission is free and advance registration is not required. For more information please contact needhamhousingcoalition@gmail.com.

events.title: Wellesley Conservation Land Trust’s Spring Lecture—”The Role of Forests in Decarbonization”
events.description: How have the forests of New England evolved since the post-colonial period and how might they save us in the future? These are complex questions involving the ways biological, physical, and human systems interact to shape the region, including all the ways the landscape is changing due to both global forces such as climate change and local forces like land use. Central to our future is the role that forests play in decarbonization and the land use tradeoffs that may lead to net zero. Wellesley Conservation Land Trust’s Spring Lecture onathan Thompson, PhD, Director of the Harvard Forest in Petersham, MA “The Role of Forests in Decarbonization: Land-Use Tradeoffs for Net Zero” Thursday, May 21, 2026, 7:00-8:30p Wakelin Room, Wellesley Free Library Wellesley Conservation Land Trust’s Spring Lecture Jonathan Thompson, Director Wellesley Forest “The Role of Forests in Decarbonization: Land-Use Tradeoffs for Net Zero” Thursday, May 21, 2026, 7:00-8:30p Wakelin Room, Wellesley Free Library How have the forests of New England evolved since the post-colonial period and how might they save us in the future? These are complex questions involving the ways biological, physical, and human systems interact to shape the region, including all the ways the landscape is changing due to both global forces such as climate change and local forces like land use. events.title: American Revolution reenactment at Needham Town Common
events.description: For the third straight year, one of New England’s leading reenacting units, The King’s Own (4th Foot), is bringing its mix of history and fun to Needham Town Common May 30, 9 am-1 pm. See Redcoats drilling and preparing for war. Learn about equipment, organization, tactics, and weapons. Come face to face with soldiers sent to prevent or stop the American Revolution, and participate in an interactive debate on independence. Kids will have the chance to learn to march and handle (child safe!) muskets. Sponsored by the Needham Council for Arts and Culture.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: The British Are Coming! On Saturday, May 30th Needham will once again host The King’s Own, a group of reenactors who portray the British Redcoats who sought to prevent the American Revolution. Adults, teens, and children are invited to attend the fun, educational event that helps commemorate the 250th Anniversary of the founding of our country. See authentic uniforms, weapons, and drill, ask questions, and learn about the challenging days that led ordinary people of Needham into direct confrontation with their King and his army. Learn how British soldiers lived, fought, and made their way though seven years of warfare from Canada to the Caribbean. This event is sponsored by the Needham Cultural Council and is free to all.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: @ The King’s Own, a group of reenactors of the British Army in 1775, will once again return to Needham for a day of interactive learning. This year is the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. See Redcoats at drill, learn what life was like in the 18th century, and dive into Needham’s history in an interactive event that should be fun and engaging for both adults and children. events.title: Hunnewell Estate Historical Tour
events.description: Saturday, May 30, 2026, 9:45 AM-12:00 PM The Tollhouse Shop, 229 Washington Street, Wellesley, MA, 02481 Space is limited! We have 24 spaces available per tour. Tickets here. Experience the rich history and vibrant present of the Hunnewell Estate with the Wellesley Historical Society’s exclusive walking tour. This 90-minute journey delves into the estate’s unique horticultural collection, offering insights into both its past and present. With only 24 spaces available per tour, this intimate experience ensures a detailed exploration of the gardens. Please note that the tour includes uneven terrain and requires all participants to use the provided transportation. Proceeds from the tour will benefit the Society’s general operating fund, supporting our mission to preserve local history.

events.title: Free movie screening of “Turning Red” at TCAN
events.description: Movie Screening: May 30th, 11am, at TCAN In celebration of Mental Health Awareness Month, the Natick Health Department is hosting a free screening of Turning Red at the TCAN Cinema Room. Turning Red introduces Mei Lee, a confident, dorky 13-year-old torn between staying her mother’s dutiful daughter and the chaos of adolescence. Her protective, if not slightly overbearing mother, Ming, is never far from her daughter—an unfortunate reality for the teenager. And as if changes to her interests, relationships and body weren’t enough, whenever she gets too excited (which is practically ALWAYS), she “poofs” into a giant red panda! Visit: turningred.com Location: 14 Summer St, Natick, MA 01760 The Center For Arts In Natick @11:00AM Register Here: https://tcan.org/events/turning-red/

events.title: Natick Garden Club Plant Sale
events.description: The Natick Garden Club is hosting its annual plant sale on Sunday, May 31 from 10am-2pm indoors at the Natick Community-Senior Center, 117 East Central St. The sale is the group’s major fundraiser for the year. They offer a variety of plants that have been proven to grow successfully in our sometimes challenging weather conditions. Most of the plants are perennials from members’ gardens, and are labeled for bloom time and season, height, spacing needed, and sun and moisture requirements. They promote native plants as well, meaning they are native to our area, and tolerant of drought conditions and excellent at providing habitat for good insects and birds. There will be a modest houseplant and seedling section. Raffle tickets for a variety of items will be available and you don’t need to be present to win. Stay in the loop by signing up for Natick Report‘s free weekday email newsletter

events.title: Craft with Wellesley Middle School Girl Scouts
events.description: Craft with Middle School Girl Scouts Sunday, May 31, 4:30 – 5:30 pm 5th – 7th graders snack & craft Adult orientation at 5:15 pm RSVP or ask questions: gstroop73505@gmail.com Fun & Friendship for Middle School Girls Wellesley Girl Scouts for 6th–8th graders combines fun, friendship, and adventure with community service. Scouts may also choose to pursue their Silver Award, a girl-led “take action” project which improves their community. Our troop meets monthly on Sundays late afternoon with one additional weekend activity scheduled each month. Participation is flexible based on your scout’s interests and schedule; our members attend various schools.This year’s activities have included: Community Service: archery and ice skating for younger girls, holiday party for Wellesley senior residents, and collecting food for Wellesley food Pantry. Scouts earned their animal helper badge by learning about the animals Unity Farm Sanctuary and crafting toys for Buddy Dog and Stray Pets in Need. Outings: apple picking, night climbing for Halloween, skiing at Wachusett, and back yard winter camping. The troop attended International Women’s Day at Mass Bay. We enjoyed the penguins at New England Aquarium and cannolis at Bova’s Bakery. The troop also bonded at beach volleyball, an arcade escape room, and cheered the Boston Legacy women’s soccer team to victory at Gillette Stadium. We will finish the year with a trip to Canobie Lake Park funded by our cookie sales. events.title: “Regenerative Gardening in Today’s World”
events.description: @ Trevor Smith, an award-winning regenerative landscape designer and a principal of CFS Regenerative Design Studios, will discuss practices that climate change has brought to home gardening: planting native species to preserve biodiversity, combatting invasive species, building healthy soils, installing green infrastructure and managing water. The program will follow a very brief Friends’ Annual Meeting on June 7, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. at the Needham Free Public Library. Mr. Smith is the founder of ReEarth Solutions, a Boston-area ecological landscape design practice. He is passionate about the natural world which inspires his commitment to sustainable landscaping practices in an era of climate change. He is also passionate about sharing his knowledge and experience and is the host of the podcast “Peace, Love & Pollinators;” the live, online, “ask the expert” event Tuesdays with Trevor; and the Hive-Mind for Business Owners, a program of the Ecological Landscape Alliance. The public is invited to this free program presented by the Friends of the Needham Public Library in the Library’s Community Room at 1139 Highland Avenue, Needham, from 2-3:30 PM, Sunday, June 7. A question-and answer period will follow the presentation. Seats are limited and registration is required. Register at https://needhamlibrary.org/events/. For more information, please contact Gay Ellen Dennett, program specialist, at gdennett@minlib.net, 781-455-7559 x223. events.title: Susan Chari
events.description: @ The Boston Scottish Fiddle Club is excited to announce that their eighth annual Gala has been set for Sunday, June 7 from 3:00pm-6:00pm at the VFW post, 20 Junction St., Needham, MA! The event incudes a Scottish music concert, Ceilidh Dancing, and a silent auction. Suggested donation is $20 at the door, kids 12 and under are free. The concert features the Boston Scottish Fiddle Orchestra, which is the performing group of the Boston Scottish Fiddle Club. Under the direction of Neil Pearlman the orchestra performs a repertoire of traditional and contemporary Scottish and Cape Breton tunes arranged to create a unique fresh sound while also keeping with the tradition. Special guest Ed Pearlman has performed, taught, and promoted the fiddle music of Scotland and Cape Breton for over 45 years throughout the US and in Canada and Scotland. He directed the Boston Scottish Fiddle Club 1981-1999, including annual Scottish Fiddle Rally concerts, and the Hatch Shell Celtic Festival, which brought together the Scottish, Cape Breton and Irish communities. He’s performed often with his wife, dancer Laura Scott, and toured and recorded with Neil Pearlman. Ed codirected music & walking tours throughout Scotland, and was the music columnist for Scottish Life magazine for 24 years. In 2024, due to his lifelong work as a performer, teacher, organizer, distributor, and writer, Ed was inducted into the Scots Trad Music Hall of Fame, a very rare honor for a non-Scot. After the concert everyone is invited to participate in the easy and fun ceilidh dances led by Laura Scott. Musicians should bring their instruments to play popular Scottish session tunes along with the ceilidh band, which will be led by Katie McNally. The Boston Scottish Fiddle Club is devoted to educating the public about traditional Scottish music and culture. Since the club’s inception in 1981 the BSFC has offered instruction in playing Scottish music via regular meetings and other activities. Currently there are monthly meetings with workshops led by well-known musicians in the field of Scottish music. The Club also holds monthly sessions on Wednesday evenings for musicians in the community to gather and play their favorite tunes together. The Boston Scottish Fiddle Orchestra, the performing group of the club, shares Scottish music with the community. Contact Information: Sue Chari Boston Scottish Fiddle Club For more information: website: bsfc.org or email: bostonscottishfiddle@bsfc.org Add to calendar Google Calendar iCalendar Outlook 365 Outlook Live Details Date: Time:

Afterwards there will some. simple ceilidh dances and participatory events. Come come down, June 7th, 3 PM at the VFW Hall in Needham. Suggested donation is $20.00 and kids under 12 are free. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, June 13 TIME: 10am-11:15am LOCATION: Meet at the Mass Bay parking lot off of Oakland Street, (not the Centennial lot) DESCRIPTION: Centennial Reservation (1.8 miles). Take a picturesque walk, through rolling meadows to the top of Maugus Hill, one of the highest points in Wellesley. View the beautiful Great Blue Hills in Canton from a hillside meadow overlooking the town of Needham. Then, descend through the meadow and around Benzanson Pond, a favorite swimming hole for dogs as well as frogs and tadpoles. Come explore this conservation land purchased by the town for Wellesley’s 1981 Centennial Celebration and now one of the most popular trails in Wellesley. This walk also includes Mass Bay land that is maintained by the Wellesley Trails Committee and the Wellesley DPW.

This year’s event is Sunday, June 7 from 10 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. at the Wellesley High School parking lot. Participants will learn how to ride safely and how to legally use critical skills training from MassBike instructors. The event includes a guided ride led by the Wellesley Police Department. During the event, helmets and bikes will be checked for proper fit and safety before moving into brief group instruction and the guided ride. Participants need a rideable bike, closed-toed shoes, and a helmet. Registration is required. (use Activity #231505).

events.title: Call for Walkers: Register for the 38th Annual Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai
events.description: Registration is open for the 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai. Scheduled for Sunday, Oct. 4, funds raised from the Walk support all forms of adult and pediatric care and cancer research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. The 2026 event will aim to raise $10 million in support of Dana-Farber’s mission to defy cancer by leading the way in groundbreaking research and lifesaving patient care. The Details: The Jimmy Fund Walk is the only organized walk permitted to use the famed Boston Marathon® course, and participants have the flexibility to choose from four distance options: 5K walk: Starting at Dana-Farber’s Longwood Medical Campus 10K walk: Starting in Newton Half Marathon walk: Starting in Wellesley Marathon walk: Starting in Hopkinton Along the route, participants will be motivated by Hero posters, photos and quotes from Dana-Farber patients, who are considered the heart and inspiration for the event. Walkers will also enjoy refreshments and cheer squads throughout the course. The event will conclude at the Boston Common Finish Line, by the corner of Charles and Beacon Streets. Finish line activities will include a celebration with food, entertainment, and more. Walkers who prefer to participate virtually can complete their chosen distance from any location. To register for the Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber or to support a walker, visit JimmyFund.org/Walk or call (866) 531-9255. Registrants can enter the promo code NEWS for $5 off the registration fee. You can also sign up to volunteer on Walk Day.
